sentences.text: Task 1. You are going to listen to five texts.
For each of them, answer the two questions given.
Mark the correct answer A, B, or C.
You have 20 seconds to look through the task.
You will hear the recording twice.
Text 1. I live in Newquay.
It's a small town on the Atlantic coast in the south of England.
It has great beaches and is remarkable
for its surfing and swimming.
It's also one of the best surfing places in the UK.
There are lots of surf schools where you can learn how to surf.
I go surfing with my friends every weekend.
I love Newquay because there are lots of other things to do as well as surfing.
If you like water sports, you can go kayaking or water skiing.
However, both of them require a lot of training.
You also have to be physically fit.
But while water skiing, you should have an experienced boatman as well
to avoid accidents.
You completely rely on the competence of the boatman.
If you like sea animals, you can also visit the Blue Reef Aquarium.

Text 2.
The outstanding Georgian painter, Elene Akhlediani, studied at Tbilisi Academy of Fine Arts.
Later, she continued her studies in Paris and spent two years in Italy.
In 1927, Akhlediani returned to her home country.
Her works are very special.
Her unique style allows the viewers to recognize her
from the works of dozens of other artists.
She uses a wide variety of shades and colors.
Over the years, the creative manner of the painter changed.
If earlier her landscapes had a golden background,
later they became more vivid and colorful.
Although she worked in all genres, Akhlediani is primarily a landscape painter.
Elene Akhlediani loved to paint the views of Tbilisi itself.
She also painted the nature of Georgia, its towns and the parts of old districts.
Many paintings by Elene Akhlediani are preserved in private collections both in Georgia and abroad.

Over the years, the creative manner of the painter changed.
If earlier her landscapes had a golden background,
later they became more vivid and colorful.
Although she worked in all genres,
Akhlediani is primarily a landscape painter.
Elene Akhlediani loved to paint the views of Tbilisi itself.
She also painted the nature of Georgia, its towns and the parts of old districts.
Many paintings by Elene Akhlediani are preserved in private collections,
both in Georgia and abroad.
Text three.
Dolphins are undoubtedly some of the most admired and well-known ocean animals in the world.
With their cute smiles, incredible acrobatic skills, and likeness to humans, dolphins have caught the attention of people.
Since ancient times, dolphins have been considered highly intelligent.
The evolution of their large brains is surprisingly similar to humans.
In fact, they are said to be the second most intelligent mammals after humans.
Dolphins can recognize themselves in a mirror and call each other unique names.
The more we learn about dolphins, the more we realize that their society is more complex than people imagined before.
Dolphins have developed some incredible abilities.
They look after other dolphins when they are ill, care for pregnant mothers, and protect the weakest in the community, as we do.
Dolphins are social animals and live in groups called schools.

Today, people face a lot of environmental problems mainly caused by pollution.
Different materials pollute the air, water, and oxygen.
and soil and affect human health. We all know that humans are the main cause of air pollution.
They burn natural gas, petroleum, and coal, and thus pollute the air. However,
pollution also happens when animals breathe or when volcanoes break out. According to a study,
air pollution is responsible for 4% of all deaths in the United States. The gas produced by the
industry and automobiles is a major source of air pollution. Aerosols, for example, can have a
serious effect on the ozone layer. We all know that the ozone layer protects life on Earth
from a variety of threats. It is our responsibility to protect the environment.
We should do our best to make the world a better place.

Text 5. Recently, while excavating Egypt's famous
pyramids, archaeologists have found pots of honey. The honey, dating back approximately
3,000 years, is the world's earliest known example. Despite this fact, it is safe to be eaten,
even today. That's because honey contains special elements allowing it to never get old.
The ancient Egyptians used honey for a lot of purposes. There are some Egyptian drawings that
show ancient beekeeping.
Thanks to them, we know that humans have worked with bees for thousands of years. But until now,
we didn't know when the beekeeping practice started. Recently, scientists discovered that
humans have been using honey and bee products for at least 9,000 years. Researchers from the
University of Bristol analyzed ancient pots and found that bee products were used as far back as
the Stone Age. Recently, while excavating Egypt's famous pyramids, archaeologists have found that
honey contains special elements allowing it to never get old. The ancient Egyptians used honey for a lot of

Hello everybody.
Welcome to Paris Sightseeing Tour.
I'm your tour guide today, and my name is Adil.
We'll discover some of the most beautiful sights of this fantastic city with our 5R Paris Bus Tour.
We'll visit the Eiffel Tower, Notre-Dame de Paris and the Louvre Museum.

a 3-hour bus tour to the Musée d'Orsay, known for its rich collection of Impressionist art,
and a 4R cruise on the Seine River.
Let's get started.
We're now heading towards the Eiffel Tower, a global cultural icon of Paris.
With 7 million visitors a year,
the Eiffel Tower is the most visited monument in the world.
For comparison, the Empire State Building in New York attracts only 3.5 million visitors a year.
The Eiffel Tower is named after the engineer Gustav Eiffel,
whose company designed and built the tower in 1889.
The tower is 324 meters tall and held the title of the tallest man-made structure of the world
until the Chrysler Building in New York was built in 1930.
Before the Eiffel Tower was built, the Washington Memorial in the United States
had the title of the highest man-made construction.
An amazing fact about the Eiffel Tower is that during winter it shrinks,
becomes smaller, by a few centimeters.
One more interesting fact about the Eiffel Tower is that, initially she was painted yellow.
Later, she was repainted red-brown.
Yes, you've heard correctly.
Parisians call the tower she, as if it were a wall.

The cathedral was the site of the coronation of Napoleon I,
and the funerals of many presidents of the French Republic.

beautiful sculptures,

We're all looking forward to seeing this beautiful monument in its full glory.

the Louvre became a museum.

who appears outside,
in the two Larise gardens.
